paragraph_or_text_span: Document Title: Standard for Day Surgery/Procedure Service Document Ref. Number: DOH/STD/HFS/DS-PS/V1/2024 Version: V1 New / Revised: New Publication Date: October 2024 Effective Date: March 2025 Document Control: DoH Strategy Sector Applies To: DoH licensed Healthcare facilities provide day surgery/procedure service. Owner: Healthcare Facilities Sector Revision Date: October 2027 Revision Period:

1.Standard Scope 1.1. The purpose of this standard is to set the requirements for Day Surgery/Procedure Services to ensure the highest levels of safety and quality for patients in the Emirate of Abu Dhabi. 1.2. This standard applies to all DoH licensed healthcare facilities providing Day Surgery/Procedure Service. 2. Definitions and Abbreviations No. Term / Abbreviation Definition 2.1 AAMEN 2.2 2.

3.Standard Requirements and Specifications 3.1 Day Surgery/Procedure Services may include but not limited to 3.1.1 Surgical procedures, particularly ENT, Dental, Plastic Surgery, Ophthalmology 3.1.2 Endoscopy - gastrointestinal, respiratory, urology. 3.1.3 Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT) for mental health inpatients 3.1.4 Day medical procedures including intravenous infusions and minor treatments

3.4.5 All relevant DoH licensed Healthcare Professionals to have completed the Information and Cyber Security Awareness courses assigned to them through Abu Dhabi Healthcare Cyberlearning Program. 3.5 Facility Design: 3.5.1 The HF shall meet the HF requirement as per the DoH Health Facility Guidelines. 3.5.2 The facility shall have access to or include one or more operating rooms (or procedure roo

post-operative site, complications, refraining from certain activities); and arrangements for enquiries or assistance outside operating hours. 3.7.1.3.15 Transfer of patients to hospital for those who are not fit to be discharged home after the procedure or anesthesia and those who have intraoperative or post operative acute complications. 3.7.1.3.16 Assessment and management of sepsis and septic

3.7.3.2 An initial assessment process is used to identify the health care needs of all patients 3.7.3.3 There is an established reassessment process for patients requiring additional services or ongoing care. 3.7.3.4 The time frame for initial assessments and, as appropriate, reassessment is consistent with each patient’s needs, organizational policy, and accepted professional guidelines. 3.7.4 Ca

3.7.7.2 A patient’s post-anesthesia status is monitored and documented, and the patient is discharged from the recovery area by qualified staff using established criteria in a setting that is sufficiently staffed and equipped to provide post-anesthesia care. 3.7.7.3 The physiological state of each patient following sedation or anesthesia is regularly checked and recorded in the patient's medical f

3.7.10 Patient education 3.7.10.1 The healthcare needs of patients and their families determine the education that is needed. These needs may include but are not limited to the nature of the patient's disease; necessary treatments; infection control procedures; safe medication, food, and nutrition use; use of medical equipment; and preoperative and postoperative care. 3.7.10.2 Education is provide
